CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC. DBA BRYMAN COLLEGE AND EVEREST COLLEGE SCHEWIZAMELLO, LISA HARDY, DIANE KELLER, JULIE "JULES"
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-28-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CORINTHIAN COLLEGES, INC, D.B.A. BRYMAN COLLEGE, LISA SCHEWIZAMELLO, DIANE HARDY AND JULIE KELLER'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE APPLICABILITY OF THE COMMERICAL RULES WAS ADDRESSED IN CASE NUMBER 464469 AS WAS PLAINTIFF'S STATUS AS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE COURT ORDERED THE MATTER TO ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFF DID NOT CHALLENGE THE RULING BUT DISMISSED THE COMPLAINT. NOW PLAINTIFF FILES AN ACTION ALLEGING SHE IS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AND ASSERTS THE A.A.A. RULES OF COMMERICIAL ARBITRATION ARE PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF IS COLLATERALLY ESTOPPED TO RE-LITIGATE THIS ISSUE. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE A.A.A. RULES ARE NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE ARBITRATOR HAS DISCRETION TO ORDER ALL FORMS OF DISCOVERY. BY STRIKING THE LAST TWO SENTENCES OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE, OTHER OBJECTIONS ARE CURED. COURT FURTHER ORDERS: THE INSTANT ACTION IS HEREBY STAYED EFFECTIVE TODAY, UNTIL AN ARBITRATION IS HAD AND COMPLETED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THIS ORDER, OR UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT EXCEPT THAT DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A MOTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 128.7 AND THE COURT WILL HEAR THAT MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

APPEARANCES: PAUL D. HERBERT, ESQUIRE (PAYNE & FEARS, 415.277.2250) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES.
RULING: DISCUSSED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE APPLICABILITY OF THE COMMERICAL RULES WAS ADDRESSED IN CASE NUMBER 464469 AS WAS PLAINTIFF'S STATUS AS AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR. THE COURT ORDERED THE MATTER TO ARBITRATION. PLAINTIFF DID NOT CHALLENGE THE RULING BUT DISMISSED THE COMPLAINT. NOW PLAINTIFF FILES AN ACTION ALLEGING SHE IS AN INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR AND ASSERTS THE A.A.A. RULES OF COMMERICIAL ARBITRATION ARE PROCEDURALLY AND SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF IS COLLATERALLY ESTOPPED TO RE-LITIGATE THIS ISSUE. THE COURT ALSO FINDS THAT THE A.A.A. RULES ARE NOT SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. THE ARBITRATOR HAS DISCRETION TO ORDER ALL FORMS OF DISCOVERY. BY STRIKING THE LAST TWO SENTENCES OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE, OTHER OBJECTIONS ARE CURED. COURT FURTHER ORDERS: THE INSTANT ACTION IS HEREBY STAYED EFFECTIVE TODAY, UNTIL AN ARBITRATION IS HAD AND COMPLETED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THIS ORDER, OR UNTIL FURTHER ORDER OF THIS COURT EXCEPT THAT DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A MOTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P.
